title: Lohagarh Fort
tagline: Lohagarh Fort — heritage city of Rajasthan

overview.short: Lohagarh Fort is a much-loved heritage city in Rajasthan, known for forts and architecture.
overview.description: Lohagarh Fort (transl. Iron Fort) is an 18th-century fort located at Bharatpur in Rajasthan, India.Maharaja Suraj Mal commissioned its construction in 1732. The British army attempted to conquer this fort 13 times. The Lohagarh Fort withstood repeated attacks of British forces led by Lord Lake during the Siege of Bharatpur in January and February 1805 when they failed in all four attempts to storm the fortress, However the Fort was successfully breached by the British East India Company on January 18, 1826, under the command of Lord Combermere, This victory during the Second Siege of Bharatpur

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Bharatpur (1825–1826)
topPlaces.description: The siege of Bharatpur was a siege that took place in the Indian princely state of Bharatpur (now part of Rajasthan) between December 1825 and January 1826. British troops under Lord Combermere initially surrounded the state's capital until on 18 January 1826 its fortress was stormed and captured.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Bharatpur (1805)
topPlaces.description: The siege of Bharatpur took place between 2 January and 22 February 1805 in the Indian Princely state of Bharatpur (now part of Rajasthan), during the Second Anglo-Maratha War. Forces of the British East India Company, led by General Gerard Lake, were repulsed four times in their attempts to storm the fortress.

topPlaces.name: Bijwari
topPlaces.description: Bijwari is a village in Wair tehsil of Bharatpur in Rajasthan, India. It is a village of Indolia gotra Jats.

topPlaces.name: Bharatpur State
topPlaces.description: Bharatpur was a tributary of the House of Scindia, and later a princely state under British suzerainty. It was ruled by the Sinsinwar clan of the Hindu Jats.

topPlaces.name: Bharatpur, Rajasthan
topPlaces.description: Bharatpur is a city in the Indian state of Rajasthan, 189 km (117 mi) south of India's capital, New Delhi, 184 km (114 mi) from Rajasthan's capital Jaipur, 56 km (35 mi) west of Agra of Uttar Pradesh and 36 km (22 mi) from Mathura of Uttar Pradesh. It is the administrative headquarters of Bharatpur District and the headquarters of Bharatpur Division of Rajasthan State.

topPlaces.name: Bharatpur Junction
topPlaces.description: Bharatpur Junction is a railway station located on the Mathura–Kota section of the New Delhi–Mumbai main line of Indian Railways in Bharatpur, Rajasthan. It is an important station of the West Central Railway zone (WCR) and has the status of Grade A station.

topPlaces.name: Keoladeo National Park
topPlaces.description: Keoladeo National Park, or Keoladeo Ghana National Park, is a national park in Rajasthan, India. The national park hosts thousands of native, resident and migratory birds, especially during the winter season, when many different species fly to the Indian subcontinent to escape harsh winters further north in Eurasia.

items.note: The siege of Bharatpur was a siege that took place in the Indian princely state of Bharatpur (now part of Rajasthan) between December 1825 and January 1826. British troops under Lord Combermere initially surrounded the state's capital until on 18 January 1826 its fortress was stormed and captured.

The siege of Bharatpur took place between 2 January and 22 February 1805 in the Indian Princely state of Bharatpur (now part of Rajasthan), during the Second Anglo-Maratha War. Forces of the British East India Company, led by General Gerard Lake, were repulsed four times in their attempts to storm the fortress.
Bijwari is a village in Wair tehsil of Bharatpur in Rajasthan, India. It is a village of Indolia gotra Jats.
Bharatpur was a tributary of the House of Scindia, and later a princely state under British suzerainty. It was ruled by the Sinsinwar clan of the Hindu Jats.

Bharatpur is a city in the Indian state of Rajasthan, 189 km (117 mi) south of India's capital, New Delhi, 184 km (114 mi) from Rajasthan's capital Jaipur, 56 km (35 mi) west of Agra of Uttar Pradesh and 36 km (22 mi) from Mathura of Uttar Pradesh. It is the administrative headquarters of Bharatpur District and the headquarters of Bharatpur Division of Rajasthan State.

Bharatpur Junction is a railway station located on the Mathura–Kota section of the New Delhi–Mumbai main line of Indian Railways in Bharatpur, Rajasthan. It is an important station of the West Central Railway zone (WCR) and has the status of Grade A station.

Keoladeo National Park, or Keoladeo Ghana National Park, is a national park in Rajasthan, India. The national park hosts thousands of native, resident and migratory birds, especially during the winter season, when many different species fly to the Indian subcontinent to escape harsh winters further north in Eurasia.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Lohagarh Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Lohagarh Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Lohagarh Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Agra Airport (Pandit Deen Dayal Upadhyay) (47 km) and the nearest railway station is Agra Cantt (50 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Lohagarh Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Lohagarh Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Lohagarh Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Lohagarh Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Lohagarh Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Siege of Bharatpur (1825–1826), Siege of Bharatpur (1805), Bijwari, Bharatpur State, Bharatpur, Rajasthan.
